# Deck

## The most capable AI assistant with its own inbox

## The most capable AI assistant with its own inbox

AI Chief Of Staff

Deck is an always-on AI assistant with its own email inbox. CC it on any thread or hand it a task, and it takes over. It learns your preferences and priorities over time, proactively flags what needs your attention, and can run on a schedule to deliver briefings and status updates automatically. It's built to take ambiguous, multi-step tasks head on, so you can move on with your day.

Subscribe [...] Hey all, I'm Stasia, founder of Deck. Deck is an AI teammate for busy operators, professionals, and teams. You can email it, use it in the app, sign it up for services you use, email alerts/newsletters, or set it up to run recurring work. It can read project files, summarize messy context, generate or edit decks/memos/sheets, answer questions over your knowledge base, and proactively surface things that need attention. Importantly, it doesn't require blanket inbox authorization to be effective and only emails you. We made email a first-class interface because delegation already happens there and it's a decades old, hardened protocol. People forward a thread, CC someone, attach files, subscribe it to email alerts and newsletters and ask for help. We believe Deck fits that behavior instead [...] Launch Team / Built With

results.title: Verdent: Your AI Technical Cofounder
results.content:
433 views

•3 reviews

Verdent Deck presents a truly fascinating and powerful approach to the AI coding assistant market. The core idea of coordinating multiple, specialized AI agents to tackle complex coding tasks in parallel is a significant differentiator that looks set to drastically cut down on development time, especially for tasks that typically require a lot of back-and-forth iteration. For a tool handling such ambitious parallel processing, the interface shown in the demo is impressively clean and intuitive. This level of execution and technical ambition suggests Verdent Deck has huge potential, and I'm excited to see how it scales to handle larger and more ambiguous codebases, making it an indispensable tool for accelerating complex feature development. Congrats on the launch! [...] 5.0

Based on 6 reviews

Review Verdent?

Reviews praise Verdent Deck for speeding up development by coordinating multiple AI agents in parallel, helping users plan tasks, catch issues early, and ship results faster. Users highlight a smooth, modern interface that stays out of the way, with clear insights and streamlined reviews/diffs. Many report meaningfully improved workflow and reduced context switching; a few note balance/tuning could improve. Overall sentiment frames it as powerful for complex, iterative work, with strong execution, high polish, and promising scalability for larger, more ambiguous codebases.

#### What's great

> Verdent Deck moves beyond the "IDE Agent" or "CI Agent" model to deliver a ground-breaking DevX. I am confident that this software is the most underrated tool on the market. You would be unwise to overlook it.

There is an elephant in the room that is harming AI adoption outside of the West Coast bubble:

• You wouldn't want to share one IDE instance across a team of several people.

• You wouldn't want to be working on concurrent high-impact move-fast features with team members who only show a pair of eyes on a GitHub PR, then send back a massive response after a dozen minutes.
